reforms.description: To increase STEM education attainment, the government emphasizes the need for improved access to quality secondary education to better prepare students for tertiary studies in STEM fields.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing STEM education is vital for building a skilled workforce that can drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Tertiary education attainment in STEM fields needs to be increased.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it targets the foundational education necessary for future innovation and economic competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: An important step in doing so is improving access to quality secondary education so that students are better prepared for STEM tertiary studies.

reforms.description: The American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 included additional funds for states to expand the services provided by One-Stop-Career-Centers, enhancing re-employment services for the unemployed.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing re-employment services can improve job matching and increase the employability of the workforce, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Act included provisions for expanding services at One-Stop-Career-Centers.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of re-employment services represents a significant investment in human capital development, which is crucial for long-term economic recovery.
reforms.source_quote: The American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 included additional funds for states to expand the services provided by One-Stop-Career-Centers.

reforms.description: The Administration’s FY2013 budget proposal included measures aimed at investing in a more comprehensive activation system for re-employment services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in a comprehensive activation system can enhance the effectiveness of re-employment services, leading to better job outcomes and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Administration’s FY2013 budget proposal included several measures aimed at investing in a more comprehensive activation system.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposal represents a significant step towards improving the effectiveness of re-employment services, which is critical for addressing long-term unemployment.
reforms.source_quote: The Administration’s FY2013 budget proposal included several other measures aimed at investing in a more comprehensive activation system.

reforms.description: The federal government plans to increase federally-funded R&D to support innovation and economic growth, countering potential reductions mandated by the Budget Control Act of 2011.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased federal R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity and stimulate economic growth through higher productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Plans to increase federally-funded R&D have been outlined in the recommendations.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing the federal R&D budget, which is crucial for maintaining the US's competitive edge in innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Government plans to increase federally-funded R&D should be implemented.

reforms.description: The proposal to establish a national innovation agency aims to create a dedicated body for coordinating and developing innovation policy in the United States.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: A national innovation agency would enhance the effectiveness of innovation policy, leading to better resource allocation and improved innovation outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The establishment of a national innovation agency has been recommended to improve innovation policy coordination.
reforms.importance_rationale: Creating a national innovation agency represents a fundamental shift in how innovation policy is structured and implemented in the US.
reforms.source_quote: Establish a national innovation agency to recognize that innovation is a national priority.

reforms.description: The Middle Class Tax Relief and Job Creation Act of 2012 introduced a requirement for Extended Unemployment Compensation recipients to receive eligibility assessments and re-employment services, including skills assessments and a re-employment plan.
reforms.package_name: Middle Class Tax Relief and Job Creation Act
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the skills and employability of unemployed individuals, the reform aims to reduce long-term unemployment and improve overall labor market outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The Act was passed and included new requirements for unemployment compensation recipients.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in the approach to unemployment compensation, focusing on re-employment services, which can have a meaningful impact on labor market participation.
reforms.source_quote: The Middle Class Tax Relief and Job Creation Act of 2012 added a requirement that all Extended Unemployment Compensation (EUC) recipients receive eligibility assessments and re-employment services.

reforms.description: The American Jobs Act proposed a tax credit for employers who hire new employees, particularly those who have been unemployed for an extended period, aiming to stimulate job creation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing employers to hire, the tax credit aims to reduce unemployment and stimulate economic activity, thereby supporting growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit was part of the proposals outlined in the American Jobs Act.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed tax credit represents a significant policy tool aimed at addressing unemployment, with potential positive effects on job creation.
reforms.source_quote: The tax credit proposed in the American Jobs Act (AJA) appears more promising than the HIRE Act subsidy because it is targeted on net increases in payrolls.

reforms.description: The Race to the Top (RTT) programme encourages US states to adopt internationally benchmarked standards and assessments, recruit effective teachers, and build data systems to measure student success.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving educational standards and teacher effectiveness, the RTT programme aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The RTT programme was launched in 2010 to promote educational reforms across states.
reforms.importance_rationale: The RTT programme represents a significant shift in educational policy aimed at improving standards and outcomes across the US education system, with potential long-term economic benefits.
reforms.source_quote: The RTT programme encourages US states to change their aspirations and organisational culture by adopting internationally benchmarked state-developed standards and assessments.

reforms.description: The establishment of a national innovation agency is proposed to maintain a focus on evidence-based innovation policy and strategy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: A national innovation agency is expected to streamline and enhance the effectiveness of innovation policies, thereby promoting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The chapter concludes with a call to establish a national innovation agency.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of a national innovation agency represents a structural change in the governance of innovation policy, aiming to enhance coordination and effectiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The chapter concludes with a call to establish a national innovation agency.

reforms.description: The US government aims to increase federal R&D spending to more than 3% of GDP, emphasizing the importance of stable funding for productive R&D activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation and productivity, leading to higher economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The President set a goal in April 2009 to devote more than 3% of GDP to R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in federal R&D funding policy, aiming for a substantial increase in investment that could transform the innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The Administration’s effort to reach the goal that the President set in April 2009 of devoting more than 3% of GDP to R&D, both public and private.

reforms.description: The Small Business Innovation Research Program (SBIR) is designed to encourage innovation-based start-ups by providing funding linked to public sector customer requirements.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By funding early-stage R&D, the SBIR program aims to stimulate the creation of new firms and promote innovation, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The SBIR program is worth over USD 2 billion annually and requires government agencies to set aside funds for it.
reforms.importance_rationale: The SBIR program is a significant initiative aimed at fostering innovation in small businesses, although it operates within existing frameworks rather than representing a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The Small Business Innovation Research Program (SBIR), which is worth over USD 2 billion annually is aimed at encouraging innovation-based start-ups.

reforms.description: The America Invents Act, passed in 2011, aims to increase patent quality by introducing new procedures for challenging patent validity and allowing the USPTO to prioritize certain applications, which may lead to quicker resolution of patent disputes and improved patent quality.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving patent quality and reducing the backlog of applications, the Act encourages innovation and investment in new technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The Act was passed into law in 2011 and has been in effect since then.
reforms.importance_rationale: The America Invents Act represents a significant overhaul of the US patent system, aiming to enhance patent quality and streamline processes, which is crucial for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Passed in 2011, the America Invents Act aims to increase patent quality by providing for new procedures for challenging patent validity.

reforms.description: The American Recovery and Reinvestment Act (ARRA) of 2009 provided a significant boost to federal R&D expenditures, including funding for the Department of Energy’s Advanced Research Projects Agency – Energy (ARPA-E), which supports high-risk energy technology projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for high-risk energy technologies can lead to breakthroughs that enhance energy efficiency and reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
reforms.status_evidence: The ARRA was enacted in 2009 and included substantial funding for various R&D initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The ARRA represents a major investment in R&D, particularly in energy technologies, which is critical for long-term innovation and addressing climate change.
reforms.source_quote: The ARRA included USD400 million of funding for the Department of Energy’s (DOE’s) Advanced Research Projects Agency – Energy (ARPA – E).

reforms.description: The Administration proposed the Advanced Manufacturing Partnership to focus approximately USD 500 million of existing programme funds on improving manufacturing performance, including the development of advanced materials and energy-efficient manufacturing processes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing manufacturing capabilities, the initiative aims to boost productivity and innovation in the sector, which can lead to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The President announced the initiative in June 2011.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in manufacturing innovation, aiming to revitalize the sector and enhance competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: In June 2011 the President announced the Advanced Manufacturing Partnership to focus approximately USD 500 million of existing programme funds on improving manufacturing performance.

reforms.description: The President proposed reforms to the Research and Experimentation (R&E) tax credit to increase its value and make it permanent, aiming to enhance business R&D investments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the tax credit and simplifying its use, the reform is expected to incentivize more firms to invest in R&D, thereby fostering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal was included in the President’s FY2013 budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a major change to the R&E tax credit system, which is crucial for encouraging private sector R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: The President has proposed that Congress consider changes in the federal tax code to encourage manufacturers to produce in the United States.

reforms.description: The Administration's National Network for Manufacturing Innovation (NNMI) aims to enhance collaboration among industry, academia, and government to drive manufacturing innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering collaboration and innovation in manufacturing, the NNMI is expected to enhance productivity and competitiveness in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The NNMI was announced as part of the Administration's efforts to support manufacturing innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The NNMI represents a significant structural change in how manufacturing innovation is supported in the U.S., aiming to enhance collaboration across sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The Administration’s new National Network for Manufacturing Innovation (NNMI) is to be structured very much along these lines.

reforms.description: The federal government proposed the establishment of a national innovation agency to enhance coherence, continuity, and coordination in innovation policy development and implementation, addressing the lack of a dedicated agency for national innovation policy in the United States.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: A dedicated agency would improve the effectiveness of innovation policy, leading to better resource allocation and enhanced productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal for a national innovation agency was part of the Startup America initiative launched in 2011.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a national innovation agency represents a structural change in the governance of innovation policy, aiming to enhance coordination and effectiveness across various government agencies.
reforms.source_quote: Establish a national innovation agency to enhance coherence, continuity and coordination in innovation policy development and implementation.

reforms.description: The federal government proposed to encourage innovation-based entrepreneurship by increasing access to capital for young firms and limiting non-compete covenants in employment contracts, which have been shown to hinder start-up activity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing access to capital and reducing barriers for entrepreneurs can stimulate start-up activity, leading to higher innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: These proposals fall within the scope of the Startup America initiative launched by the Administration in 2011.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance the entrepreneurial ecosystem, although it builds on existing frameworks rather than creating a new structural change.
reforms.source_quote: Encourage innovation-based entrepreneurship by increasing access to capital that supports young firms and by limiting non-compete covenants in employment contracts.

reforms.description: To foster innovation and economic growth, reductions in the federal R&D budget should be as limited as possible, ideally continuing the funding path established in the 2007 America COMPETES Act to double budgets for key science agencies within a decade.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Sustaining R&D funding is crucial for maintaining innovation capacity and supporting long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation emphasizes limiting reductions in the federal R&D budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to R&D funding, which is essential for sustaining the US's innovative edge and addressing future challenges.
reforms.source_quote: Given the importance of R&D for innovation and economic growth, reductions in the federal R&D budget should be as limited as possible.

reforms.description: Patent reform should be advanced beyond the America Invents Act to ensure that legal standards for injunctive relief and damages for patent infringement align with realistic business practices.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving patent laws can enhance the innovation environment by providing clearer protections and incentives for inventors and businesses.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation calls for further patent reform beyond the America Invents Act.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is critical for enhancing the patent system, which plays a vital role in fostering innovation and protecting intellectual property.
reforms.source_quote: Patent reform (America Invents Act) needs to be taken further by ensuring that the legal standards for granting injunctive relief and damages awards for patent infringement reflect realistic business practices.

reforms.description: Measures proposed by the Administration to strengthen manufacturing competitiveness should be implemented, recognizing the spillover benefits from manufacturing activity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening manufacturing can lead to increased innovation and productivity, benefiting the overall economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation emphasizes the need to implement measures to strengthen manufacturing competitiveness.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it targets a key sector of the economy that has broad implications for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The measures proposed by the Administration to strengthen manufacturing competitiveness should be implemented.

reforms.description: Education reform is needed to strengthen achievement and address lagging tertiary attainment in STEM fields.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing education in STEM fields is essential for building a skilled workforce that can drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Education reform is needed to strengthen achievement and to address lagging tertiary attainment in the fields of science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM).
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is crucial for ensuring a future workforce capable of meeting the demands of an increasingly technology-driven economy.
reforms.source_quote: Education reform is needed to strengthen achievement and to address lagging tertiary attainment in the fields of science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM).

reforms.description: The Administration has proposed to achieve USD1 trillion in discretionary spending savings over the next 10 years through budgetary caps and various measures, including cuts in defense and military outlays, while protecting spending for research and education that enhances long-term growth prospects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Protecting research and education funding is essential for fostering innovation and long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Administration's budget proposal outlines these discretionary spending savings.
reforms.importance_rationale: This proposal represents a significant shift in budgetary priorities, emphasizing the importance of research and education funding for future growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Administration has proposed to achieve USD1 trillion in discretionary spending savings over the next 10 years through the budgetary caps established by the Budget Control Act.

reforms.description: The proposal to appropriate funds approved in the 2007 America COMPETES Act aims to double the funding of key basic research agencies, enhancing the stability and productivity of R&D funding in the United States.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for basic research agencies is expected to enhance innovation capacity and technological advancements, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal to appropriate the funds was mentioned as preferable for enhancing R&D productivity.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing the funding landscape for basic research, which is crucial for long-term innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: It would be preferable to cut non-R&D expenditures... by appropriating the funds approved in the 2007 America COMPETES Act, which called for doubling the funding of three key basic research agencies.

reforms.description: The America Invents Act of 2011 introduced significant reforms to the patent system, aiming to improve patent quality and reduce review times, thereby encouraging innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the efficiency and quality of the patent system, the Act is expected to stimulate innovation and investment in new technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The Act was passed and implemented to address concerns about the patent system's performance.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a fundamental change in the patent system, which is critical for fostering innovation and protecting intellectual property rights.
reforms.source_quote: Some of the major concerns about the performance of the US patent system were addressed in the America Invents Act of 2011.

reforms.description: The establishment of a national innovation office is proposed to enhance coherence and continuity in the implementation of the national innovation strategy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: A national innovation office would streamline efforts and resources towards innovation, potentially leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to establish a national innovation office was included in the proposals for strengthening innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the establishment of an office is a structural change that supports existing innovation efforts rather than a direct funding or policy overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Establish a national innovation office to increase coherence and continuity in implementation of the national innovation strategy.

reforms.description: The SBIR programme is recommended for continuous evaluation to assess its economic impact and improve performance, emphasizing the need for better project management and tracking of award winners.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the effectiveness of the SBIR programme can lead to better outcomes for firms, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The need for continuous evaluation of the SBIR programme has been highlighted.
reforms.importance_rationale: The recommendation for continuous evaluation represents a significant step towards improving an existing programme that supports innovation.
reforms.source_quote: All of these studies concur on the need for a continuous effort to carefully evaluate the SBIR programme to assess its real economic impact.

reforms.description: Federal R&D support programmes are urged to adapt to the changing nature of innovation, including open innovation and service sector innovation, to better support economic growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning R&D programmes with modern innovation practices, the potential for increased productivity and economic growth is enhanced.
reforms.status_evidence: Federal R&D support programmes should recognize the changing nature of innovation and adapt accordingly.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses the need for federal programmes to evolve, which is crucial for maintaining competitiveness in a rapidly changing innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Federal R&D support programmes should recognize the changing nature of innovation and adapt accordingly.
